channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rome's iconic Trevi Fountain reopens after renovation work in time for the Jubilee Holy Year
item.description: ROME (AP) — Rome’s iconic Trevi Fountain reopened on Sunday ahead of the inauguration of the 2025 Jubilee Holy Year, following the completion of extraordinary maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What are 'snow rollers'? Photos of rare phenomenon in Michigan spark curiosity
item.description: “This is the coolest thing I've ever seen,” one viewer wrote, remarking on the dozens of doughnut-shaped snowballs peppered across a field.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NORAD's Santa tracker was a Cold War morale boost. Now it attracts millions of kids
item.description: The Christmas tradition has become nearly global in scope: Children from around the world track Santa Claus as he sweeps across the earth, delivering presents and defying time.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson was solid in Baltimore's biggest game of the season so far. Then, after one of the star quarterback's rare slip-ups, one of his teammates delivered the play of the day.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 Party City stores will stay open despite national closures — here's where
item.description: Party City stores in Hawaii will remain open, even as the national chain announced plans to close 850 locations across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Lopez, Fox 5

item.title: Senate passes RFK Stadium legislation in major suprise
item.description: The legislation had been removed from a continuing resolution to fund the government, which seemed to doom it.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, KRON4

item.title: Texans WR Tank Dell hospitalized after severe knee injury on TD catch vs. Chiefs
item.description: Texans wide receiver Tank Dell was taken to the University of Kansas Medical Center and the team said he would remain overnight after sustaining a severe left knee injury while making a 30-yard touchdown catch in a 27-19 loss to the Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: It's eggnog season. The boozy beverage dates back to medieval England but remains a holiday hit
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — At Scoma’s Restaurant in San Francisco, this holiday season 's batch of eggnog began 11 months ago. The process typically starts in late January, just after the previous year's celebrations are over.
item.dc:creator: TERRY CHEA, STEPHEN SMITH and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deal spares federal workers before Christmas but there's concern about possible job cuts under Trump
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Johnny Zuagar said he tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighed how much to spend on Christmas presents. “I’ve got to keep a poker face,” Zuagar, a statistician at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What NYC Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, ABC 4

item.title: NBA fines Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla $35,000 for postgame comments toward officials
item.description: The NBA fined the Boston Celtics coach $35,000 on Saturday “for aggressively pursuing and directing inappropriate language toward a game official” at the end of his team's loss to the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL star Michael Vick officially hired by Norfolk State on Friday
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Norfolk State officially named Michael Vick its head football coach Friday, the latest hire of a talented NFL player with no college coaching experience to lead an HBCU program.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's a look at the $100 billion in disaster relief in the government spending bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congress is allocating more than $100 billion in emergency aid designed to address extensive damage caused by disasters after this week's scramble to find consensus on a government spending bill.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon workers continue strike four days from Christmas
item.description: Amazon workers at a Staten Island facility are just the latest to walk off the job during the final crucial days of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 2 dead, 60 hurt after car drives into German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities suspect was an attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma man dies by lethal injection in the nation’s final execution of 2024
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — An Oklahoma man who killed a 10-year-old girl in a cannibalistic fantasy died by lethal injection Thursday in the nation’s 25th and final execution of the year. Kevin Ray Underwood was pronounced dead at 10:14 a.m. at the Oklahoma State Penitentiary in McAlester.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles deputy mayor allegedly made bomb threat to City Hall
item.description: Federal agents searched the home of Los Angeles Deputy Mayor Brian Williams on Tuesday following allegations that he made a bomb threat to City Hall earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Abbott to unveil new border security strategy
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott plans to unveil what his staff is calling a "new border security strategy" at a ranch outside Eagle Pass, Texas, on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: The egg industry kills 350 million male chicks a year. New tech offers an alternative
item.description: Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The US egg industry kills 350 million chicks a year. New technology offers an alternative
item.description: WILTON, Iowa (AP) — Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, ABC 4
